plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure and exit positions below their stops.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
A significant portion of the book will be held as cash.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets a modest ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces downside risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Drawdowns should be minimal, but growth potential is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL to exit a position below its stop, and trim SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
Buy AMD at 5% as a strong scanner setup.
Increase QQQI to 22% for a balanced income component.
This plan aims for risk-first growth with controlled leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for ~2.5%/month growth by blending broad index, quality tech, and controlled leverage. It reduces overall risk from current levels while seeking growth. Expected swings are moderate, with a cash buffer for stability.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL to exit the position below its stop, as per discipline.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
Buy AMD at 10% and SNOW at 5% to lean into high-scoring scanner setups.
Increase SPAL to 5% to utilize leveraged ETFs for aggressive growth.
This plan uses leverage and concentration for higher potential returns.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4%/month by stretching growth with leveraged ETFs and concentrated high-scoring setups. It significantly amplifies both upside and drawdown potential. Be aware of daily decay and rebalancing in leveraged ETFs; strict stop discipline is critical to manage substantial potential losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim CRWD to 5% and SMH to 5% to reduce high-beta single name and sector exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
This plan focuses on generating reliable income with low drawdown.
A significant cash buffer will be maintained for stability.
income.read: This income-focused approach targets ~1%/month by rotating into income and broad index ETFs. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable distributions over growth. While distributions are variable and NAV can fall, this mode aims for cash-like income stability.
